title: dianthus serrulatus subsp. serrulatus
intro: Dianthus serrulatus subsp. serrulatus, also known as Saw-wort, is a perennial plant that belongs to the carnation family and is native to Europe and Asia. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Saw-wort include its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive issues, as well as its potential to support respiratory health. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
